Elon even hinted at the third part of his master plan which would "simply" be scaling up.  Read master plan part 1 & 2 to get a zommed out picture & prepare for part 3.  Note: model 3 & Y is already great & cheap to make, Tesla has 30% margins on top, they just need to scale up.

PE 90 sounds high now yes, but it's just a matter of execution at this point, no speculation on development.  Tesla does have speculative development of self driving cars, but those are not needed for the valuation to be on par with Apple both in PE & market cap by 2030

Recession kicks out new players, Tesla had a decade in 2010 with lots of money & no competition.  New players will have less govt EV subsidies, higher interest on loans & Tesla + old car makers as competitors in 2020 decade. Very few will survive & none to compete seriously

I estimate Teslas product is better vs competitors than Apple was vs theirs back then  Just like Apple also had computers, laptops & earphones Tesla has solar panels, roofs, chargers & home battery storage  Tesla designs & produces their product, Apple only designs.

Tesla is aiming for 20m cars/year end of this decade.  With Elon time we'll recalculate not reaching that ambitious target & say 14m cars. Still we have the same kind of journey as Apple had from 2012 until today.  Apple went up by 9x, Tesla will likely be heading a similar path.

It's currently an interesting investment case (not advice)  Tesla PE 90 @ 760B Apple PE 23 @2300B  = needs 3x earnings to get to Apple PE and another 3x = 9x from today to get to Apple Mcap & PE.  Tesla sold ~1m cars last year, making ~1,2m cars now.  2 new factories to scale up

The reason they wait with the Semi is because all the batteries they can get are put into 3 & Y. While Tesla wait for more batteries they'll keep improving the Semi truck. It's already driving around for continuous improvement tests.

In todays dollar value terms, not only through fiat dilution.  Tesla will sell each vehicle they produce. Why? Because they are battery constrained.  If model 3 & Y market get temporarily saturated, they'll just put the batteries into the cybertruck & Tesla Semi.

Apple just continued producing, kept market share dominance & kept high prices.  Only difference is marketing.  Tesla doesn't even have a PR department. Think about how well their sales will be if (when) they do get one.   Tesla will overtake Apple as the world's largest stock.
